name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with hands-on analytical characterization, LC/GC-MS-adjacent method experience, stability analysis, and DOE-guided process-development documentation. Connects structure-property data with technical reports and reproducible methods for cross-functional development teams.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead process-development and analytical characterization workflows for bio-based polymer systems, using DOE, spectroscopy, FT-IR, and thermal analysis to define reproducible material-quality readouts.
Translate experimental results into technical reports, SOP-ready procedures, and process recommendations that support method consistency and scale-up decisions.
Apply SPC/statistical thinking to identify critical process parameters and improve reproducibility across synthesis and characterization campaigns.

Developed structure-property datasets for 15+ organic and organometallic electrochemical materials using UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, NMR, GPC, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, TGA, DSC, GC-MS, and LC-MS-adjacent methods.

Investigated stability and degradation pathways through electrochemical and spectroscopic stress testing, supporting root-cause analysis for oxidative degradation, delamination, and redox instability.
Maintained rigorous experimental notebooks, sample traceability, and method documentation for multi-technique analytical workflows.

Co-led AFOSR-funded process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings, connecting process conditions with optical/material response through spectroscopy and thin-film characterization.
Developed QCM-D-monitored polymer thin-film deposition workflows under flow, using DOE-style process variation to assess growth kinetics and coating reproducibility.
Managed operations for a 15-member process-development laboratory, implementing equipment tracking, calibration routines, SOPs, and safety protocols that improved instrument uptime by 30%.
Trained researchers on instrument workflows, experimental documentation, and safe handling while coordinating multiple concurrent materials-development projects.

projects.title: Multi-Technique Analytical Characterization
projects.description: Built structure-property datasets across functional materials using spectroscopic, electrochemical, chromatographic, and thermal techniques to assess stability and degradation.
projects.tech: UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, LC-MS/GC-MS exposure, NMR, GPC, TGA, DSC

projects.title: Stability and Degradation Root-Cause Analysis
projects.description: Identified oxidative degradation, redox instability, and interface failure modes through stress testing and analytical readouts, translating findings into mitigation recommendations.
projects.tech: Electrochemical stress testing, spectroscopy, root-cause analysis

projects.title: DOE-Guided Process Development
projects.description: Defined critical process parameters and reproducible characterization workflows for polymer process-development campaigns.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).

skills.Method Development: Experimental design, method documentation, reproducibility studies, validation mindset, sample traceability, technical reports
skills.Process & Pharma-Adjacent: DOE, SPC, process windows, stability/degradation analysis, formulation/process-development adjacency, GMP documentation awareness
skills.Data & Collaboration: Excel, Python/basic analysis, JMP/basic, cross-functional technical communication, risk assessment
